text:
Liability was established by court default before the damages trials.

In both the Connecticut and Texas cases, judges entered default judgments against Alex Jones/Infowars as a sanction for discovery and compliance failures. That meant the later jury proceedings were primarily about damages, not whether he was liable in the first place.

So the crisp answer is: court default first; juries decided damages afterward.
